name: Data store / database / storage engine
summary: A systems-software component that persists, indexes and serves data with defined durability, consistency and concurrency guarantees. Its contract is the survival and correctness of data across crashes, contention and replication rather than an end-user interface.

concerns: durability contract: exactly what a commit ack guarantees against crash/power-loss (fsync/O_DIRECT, group commit, sync vs async commit)
on-disk/page format with block checksums and corruption detection, scrubbing and repair
consistency model across replicas (linearizable, serializable, snapshot, or eventual with bounded staleness)
schema/format versioning, migration and compaction/vacuum semantics
backup consistency (physical vs logical) and what a restored image is guaranteed to represent
questions: Is the durability guarantee precisely defined (when an ack means the write survives crash/power loss)?
Are page checksums and corruption detection/repair (scrubbing) required?
Is the replica consistency model (linearizable, snapshot, eventual) specified with a bounded-staleness contract?

concerns: crash recovery via WAL/redo-undo (ARIES-style) with a bounded recovery time and checkpoint cadence
replication mode (sync/async, quorum, Raft/Paxos) with automatic failover and leader election
RPO/RTO on node, disk or zone loss
split-brain prevention, fencing and quorum loss behaviour
point-in-time recovery (PITR), backup verification and periodic restore drills
durability fault testing against power-cut, torn writes and fsync-lie storage
typical_requirements: WAL & crash-recovery guarantee with bounded recovery time
questions: Is crash recovery (WAL replay/checkpointing) and its bounded recovery time specified?
Are replication mode, automatic failover and RPO/RTO on node/zone loss defined?
Is point-in-time recovery with periodic restore-drill verification required?

concerns: throughput and latency (p50/p99) under a defined concurrency and lock/latch-contention level
read/write/space amplification bounds
index and query-plan efficiency; planner stability
buffer-pool/cache sizing and hit-ratio targets
MVCC bloat / GC and background compaction impact on foreground latency
behaviour when the working set exceeds RAM and at connection-concurrency limits
typical_requirements: OLTP/OLAP latency & throughput targets under stated concurrency
questions: Are throughput/latency targets stated under a defined concurrency and contention level?
Are read/write/space amplification and compaction/GC overheads bounded?
Is behaviour when the working set exceeds memory specified?

concerns: transaction model (ACID vs BASE) and the exact guarantees of each supported isolation level and the anomalies it permits (dirty/non-repeatable/phantom reads, write skew, lost update)
query/DML/DDL surface and standard/dialect conformance
deterministic constraint enforcement (uniqueness, foreign keys, checks, NOT NULL)
type coercion, NULL, ordering and collation semantics
atomicity and rollback boundaries of multi-statement and multi-key operations
questions: Are the supported isolation levels and the anomalies each permits (dirty/non-repeatable/phantom reads, write skew) specified?
Is the transaction model (ACID vs eventual) and its atomicity/rollback boundaries defined?
Are constraint enforcement and type/collation semantics specified?

concerns: authentication (password/SCRAM, mutual-TLS certificate, IAM) and the privilege/role model (GRANT/REVOKE, row- and column-level security)
encryption at rest (TDE) and in transit (TLS) with key management and rotation
audit logging of DDL, privilege changes and sensitive DML
injection and untrusted-input handling across the query surface
secrets/key custody and separation of the data-encryption key from the master key
questions: Are authentication and the privilege/role model (including row/column-level access) specified?
Are encryption at rest and in transit required, with key management and rotation?
Is audit logging of schema/privilege/data changes defined?

concerns: wire-protocol and client-driver compatibility and versioning
on-disk format forward/backward compatibility across upgrades, with a defined downgrade path
query-language dialect / SQL-standard conformance level
replication and backup compatibility across engine versions
rolling upgrade in a mixed-version cluster without downtime
typical_requirements: on-disk format version compatibility & rolling upgrade
questions: Are wire-protocol and on-disk format compatibility across versions (upgrade and downgrade) specified?
Is rolling upgrade in a mixed-version cluster supported?
Is the query-language dialect / standard-conformance level defined?

concerns: backup/PITR operations, retention and periodic restore drills
observability of replication lag, lock/latch waits, buffer hit ratio, WAL growth and disk headroom
online (no-downtime) schema change and index build
capacity planning and storage-growth management
runbooks for failover, table/index bloat and full-disk conditions
typical_requirements: operational metrics (replication lag, WAL, locks, disk)
questions: Are backup/PITR, retention and periodic restore drills specified?
Are key operational metrics (replication lag, lock waits, WAL/disk growth) exposed and alerted?
Is online (no-downtime) schema/index change supported?

concerns: diagnosability: slow-query logs, EXPLAIN/plan visibility and wait-event instrumentation
a consistency/linearizability fault-injection test suite (e.g. Jepsen-style)
modular, pluggable storage/index-engine boundaries
internal metrics and tracing hooks
questions: Are diagnostics (slow-query log, plan/EXPLAIN, wait events) required?
Is a consistency/fault-injection test suite (e.g. Jepsen-style) mandated?
Are internal metrics and tracing exposed?

concerns: supported OS/filesystem/architecture matrix and per-filesystem durability (fsync) assumptions
storage-backend targets (local NVMe, network/EBS-style volumes, object storage)
data-file portability across endianness and page size
packaging (containers, OS packages) and resource footprint
questions: Are supported OS/filesystem/architecture and their durability (fsync) assumptions specified?
Are the storage backends (local, network, object) defined?
Is data-file portability across endianness/page-size stated?
